pattern.name: switch_to_branch_via_known_domain_contiguity_including_wraparound
pattern.summary: A switch can be reduced to a conditional branch or unconditional branch when the condition value is known to lie in a restricted constant range, and the set of case values targeting one successor forms a contiguous interval within that known domain, allowing wraparound at the domain boundaries. If the explicitly listed cases are not contiguous in normal unsigned order, treat the known domain as circular and detect whether the taken set or its complement is contiguous. Then lower to an icmp-based branch (or direct branch if all domain values go to one destination). Single-case selections become icmp eq/ne. The chosen compare may be expressed either for the selected cases or for the complement, whichever is simpler.
preconditions: The switch has effectively two semantic destinations over the reachable domain: one destination for a set of case values and the other for the remaining reachable values (typically default).
A known value domain exists for the switch condition, from range metadata/attribute, assumes, dominating masks, or direct reasoning.
Within that known domain, either the case set for one destination or its complement is a contiguous interval, where contiguity may wrap from the upper end of the known domain to the lower end.
All values outside the known domain are unreachable or impossible, so default may be dead or reducible accordingly.
fold_strategy: Collect all reachable values in the known domain and partition by destination.
If not contiguous, check wraparound contiguity relative to the known domain boundaries.
If the chosen set is a single value, use icmp eq; if its complement is a single value, use icmp ne.
If the chosen interval covers the entire known domain, replace with an unconditional branch to that destination.

Otherwise generate a canonical unsigned range test, typically by subtracting the interval start and comparing ult against interval length; for wraparound/complement cases, choose the polarity/destination that yields the simplest test.
Preserve/update PHIs and branch weights when rewriting CFG.
notes: The motivating example has known domain [0,5] from range(i8 0,6). Cases {0,4,5} are wraparound-contiguous in that domain as [4,5] U [0,0], so the complement {1,2,3} is ordinary contiguous and the switch becomes %add = add i8 %arg, -1; %cmp = icmp ult i8 %add, 3.
This is not limited to attributes; the same reasoning applies when the known domain comes from assumes or masks.
Positive tests below focus only on cases that should optimize.

testcases.name: wrap_attr_basic
wrap_attr_same_shape_reordered_cases
wrap_attr_complement_side_cases
wrap_attr_wrapping_interval_direct
wrap_attr_single_case_eq
wrap_attr_single_case_complement_ne
wrap_attr_full_domain_to_if
wrap_attr_i1_full_domain
wrap_attr_i4_wrapping_known_domain
wrap_attr_i16_wrapping_known_domain
wrap_assume_range_basic
wrap_assume_lower_and_upper
testcases.ir:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1)

define i32 @wrap_assume_lower_and_upper(i8 %arg) {
entry:
  %lo = icmp uge i8 %arg, 10
  %hi = icmp ult i8 %arg, 16
  %both = and i1 %lo, %hi
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %both)
  switch i8 %arg, label %else [
    i8 10, label %if
    i8 14, label %if
    i8 15, label %if
  ]

if:
  ret i32 55

else:
  ret i32 66
}
testcases.expected_optimized_ir:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1)

define i32 @wrap_assume_lower_and_upper(i8 %arg) {
entry:
  %lo = icmp uge i8 %arg, 10
  %hi = icmp ult i8 %arg, 16
  %both = and i1 %lo, %hi
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %both)
  %add = add i8 %arg, -11
  %cmp = icmp ult i8 %add, 3
  br i1 %cmp, label %else, label %if

if:
  ret i32 55

else:
  ret i32 66
}

testcases.name: wrap_masked_assume_domain
testcases.expected_optimized_ir:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1)

define i32 @wrap_masked_assume_domain(i8 %arg) {
entry:
  %masked = and i8 %arg, 7
  %ok = icmp eq i8 %masked, %arg
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %ok)
  %add = add i8 %arg, -1
  %cmp = icmp ult i8 %add, 5
  br i1 %cmp, label %else, label %if

if:
  ret i32 8

else:
  ret i32 9
}

testcases.name: wrap_phi_update
wrap_attr_larger_domain_choose_selected_interval
wrap_attr_larger_domain_choose_complement_interval
wrap_attr_two_value_domain_full_coverage

src_to_expected.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @wrap_attr_full_domain_to_if(i8 %arg) {
init:
  %#range_0_%arg = !range i8 %arg, i8 0, i8 6
  br label %entry

entry:
  switch i8 %#range_0_%arg, label %else [
    i8 0, label %if
    i8 1, label %if
    i8 2, label %if
    i8 3, label %if
    i8 4, label %if
    i8 5, label %if
  ]

if:
  ret i32 5

else:
  ret i32 6
}
=>
define i32 @wrap_attr_full_domain_to_if(i8 %arg) {
entry:
  br label %if

if:
  ret i32 5
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

opt_to_expected.src:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @wrap_attr_full_domain_to_if(i8 range(i8 0, 6) %arg) {
entry:
  switch i8 %arg, label %else [
    i8 0, label %if
    i8 1, label %if
    i8 2, label %if
    i8 3, label %if
    i8 4, label %if
    i8 5, label %if
  ]

if:                                               ; preds = %entry, %entry, %entry, %entry, %entry, %entry
  ret i32 5

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i32 6
}

i8 %arg = poison

  >> Jump to %entry
